distribution_narrative: Upper Mananara and Rienana River drainages, SE highlands near Vondrozo/Vevembe; COFAV corridor
notes: Zero georeferenced GBIF records; approximate coords ~22.72S, 47.20E

distribution_narrative: Antainambalana and Vohimaro Rivers, Makira Forest Protected Area, NE Madagascar
notes: Rediscovered Sept 2023 by Re:wild in Makira Natural Park; lost since 2003. Missing from CARES Bedotiidae R4.1 list.

distribution_narrative: Mananjary River drainage, SE Madagascar; micro-endemic
notes: ZIMS + ZTL records. Missing from CARES Bedotiidae R4.1 list.

distribution_narrative: Iaroka-Rianila basin, Sandrakatrana Stream at Ampasimbe; 100-850m elevation
notes: Type locality 18d56m26sS 48d41m01sE. Missing from CARES Bedotiidae R4.1 list.

distribution_narrative: Ifontsy to Anove rivers, east coast; also Ile Sainte-Marie (Nosy Boraha)
notes: Best-documented Bedotia; 19 GBIF points. ZIMS records.

distribution_narrative: Lower Ivoloina to Manambolo Creek / Pangalanes Canal system; introduced to Betsiboka basin
notes: Citizen Conservation Category III since 2021. Missing from CARES Bedotiidae R4.1 list.

distribution_narrative: Manantenina River / Lokoho River basin, SE boundary PN de Marojejy, NE Madagascar
notes: Authority: Stiassny & Harrison 2000. PVL collection data: 14d29m82sS, 49d49m27sE, 41m asl.

distribution_narrative: Ankavanana River system, Masoala Peninsula, NE Madagascar
notes: Exceptionally well-documented; 12 GBIF points. Holotype at -15.304, 50.222.

distribution_narrative: Faraony River drainage, Manakara Province, E Madagascar; micro-endemic
notes: No captive population. CRITICAL GAP: zero GPS data for this CR species.

distribution_narrative: Ankavia-Ankavanana river system, Masoala Peninsula, NE Madagascar
notes: PVL coords: Ankavia 14d56m08sS 50d15m19sE; Ankavanana 14d49m36sS 50d06m63sE. Likely = Vences sp. Antalaha.

distribution_narrative: Bemarivo River drainage, NE Madagascar (SAVA Region)
notes: PVL coords: Nosivolo Creek at village 14d13m39sS, 50d00m36sE, 43m asl.

distribution_narrative: Fontsimavo River, Ivoloina Basin, near Betampona Strict Nature Reserve, E Madagascar
notes: PVL coords: Site 1 17d56m33sS 49d16m08sE 80m; Site 2 17d57m24sS 49d12m70sE 59m.

distribution_narrative: Lazana River at Beforona, Rianila drainage, E Madagascar
notes: PVL coords: 18d59m06sS, 48d36m75sE. = Vences sp. Beforana.

distribution_narrative: Mahanara du Nord River area, NE Madagascar
notes: PVL coords: Belandro Creek 14d05m40sS, 50d05m10sE, 11m asl.

distribution_narrative: Manombo Special Reserve area, SE Madagascar
notes: Only undescribed Bedotia with GPS data: -23.028, 47.732 (Vences 2004, 44m).

distribution_narrative: Namorona River drainage near Ranomafana, SE Madagascar
notes: PVL coords: Ambatoharana Stream 21d15m59sS, 47d27m27sE, 475m. Vences GPS: -21.354, 47.608, 468m. = Vences sp. Ranomafana.

distribution_narrative: Nosivolo River below Ambatomasina village, E Madagascar
notes: PVL coords: 20d03m55sS, 48d07m39sE, 366m asl. MNHN specimen at -20.050, 48.133.

distribution_narrative: Sambava River drainage area, NE Madagascar
notes: PVL coords: 14d18m25sS, 50d03m30sE, 18m asl.

distribution_narrative: Andrafia Stream near Ankarefo village, SE Madagascar
notes: PVL coords: 23d22m65sS, 47d24m82sE, 60m asl. Garassa is also a generic Malagasy name for Bedotia; taxonomic validity uncertain.

distribution_narrative: Lake Alaotra basin, Perinet/Andasibe area, Mangoro tributaries, Torotorofotsy wetland, Betsiboka tributaries; widest-ranging Rheocles
notes: 15+ georeferenced sites spanning 17.5S-19.1S. Katrana/Antsiriva.

distribution_narrative: Amboaboa River / Mangarahara tributary, Sofia drainage, NW Madagascar
notes: REDISCOVERED 2021 (Carvalho et al. 2024, Sci. Reports 14:20427): 331 individuals via traditional fishing. Authority corrected from Sparks 2016.

distribution_narrative: Upper Mandolotra / Nosivolo River, Mangoro drainage, E Madagascar
notes: Most range-restricted described Rheocles. Authority corrected: Stiassny & Reinthal 1992 (not 1997).

distribution_narrative: Antainambalana and Lokoho River basins near Andapa, NE Madagascar
notes: NOT extinct: 1993 collections by P. de Rham from Lokoho River (AMNH 215500, 215503). IUCN DD assessed 23 June 2016.

distribution_narrative: Mangoro River system including Nosivolo, Manambola; central-eastern Madagascar
notes: IUCN DD assessed 23 June 2016. Rediscovered near Marolambo since 1996. SAIAB 2007-2008 surveys added 2 georeferenced sites.

distribution_narrative: Lokoho River basin, Andapa region, NE Madagascar; 157-940m elevation
captive_institutions: Cologne Zoo; Toronto Zoo; 9+ European zoos; ~81 individuals in North America
notes: Only Rheocles in captivity. Holotype at -14.5825, 49.629, 446m. Vatosoa = gemstone in Malagasy.

distribution_narrative: Sandrangato River and Mamavo River, Mangoro drainage, central-eastern Madagascar
notes: GBIF adds Mamavo River locality at -19.283, 48.267 (AMNH 228077).

distribution_narrative: Ambatovy mine area and surrounding Mangoro/Rianila headwater drainages, E Madagascar; 890-990m
notes: ~40+ genus-level GBIF records from SAIAB (2007-2012). Range: ~18.73S-19.04S, 48.23E-48.65E.

distribution_narrative: Antainambalana River, Makira Natural Park, NE Madagascar
notes: Rediscovered Sept 2023 during Re:wild expedition. Lost since 2006. May be R. pellegrini.

distribution_narrative: Vakoana River, Ambodivato drainage, NE Madagascar
notes: Discovered Nov 2021 (Carvalho et al. 2024). 7 individuals captured. Genetically distinct from R. derhami. GenBank OR737848-OR737884.

distribution_narrative: Nosivolo River, ~30km stretch centered on Marolambo, E Madagascar
notes: Co-occurs with Oxylapia polli. 14 georeferenced GBIF points. One suspect coord at -19.04, 46.68 (georeferencing error).

distribution_narrative: Nosivolo River system centered on Marolambo, E Madagascar; southernmost records near Ambodivoar (-20.315S)
notes: Monotypic genus. IUCN=EN (downgraded from CR in 2016). Rheophilic: associated with rapids. 17 georeferenced GBIF records.

distribution_narrative: Widespread across Madagascar; Nosy Be crater lakes, central highlands, east coast, NW lakes; likely species complex of 3-7 species
notes: 288 GBIF records spanning -13.3S to -25.2S. Geographic clustering corresponds to undescribed CARES forms.

distribution_narrative: Andapa basin, NE Madagascar; Manantenina River, Sarahandrano Creek
notes: GBIF records filed under P. polleni: AMNH 231363 at -14.50, 49.82; AMNH 231360 at -14.47, 49.92.

distribution_narrative: Betsiboka River drainage, W Madagascar; Lake Ravelobe, Befandriana-Mandritsara area
notes: CARES 2026 R4.1

distribution_narrative: Betsileo highlands, central Madagascar; Imerina, Ambalavao, Horombe, Mananatanana River
notes: GBIF records from NHMUK, MNHN, AMNH filed under P. polleni.

distribution_narrative: East coast Madagascar; Tamatave to Mananjary
notes: East coast small spot form. Multiple GBIF records filed under P. polleni.

distribution_narrative: SW Madagascar; Tulear, Maroamalona, Lac Iotry
notes: GBIF: Tulear -23.33, 43.52 (1926 MNHN); Maroamalona -23.42, 43.83 (1926 MNHN); Lac Iotry (AMNH 1929).

distribution_narrative: Vevembe/Vondrozo area, Mananara du Sud drainage, SE Madagascar
notes: 10+ GBIF specimens from Sahapindra R., Vahifito R., Marosivy R., Ampisika R., Karianga Camp (1995-1999). Sympatric with Ptychochromoides vondrozo.

distribution_narrative: Lake Kinkony and satellite lakes (Andranobe, La Digue), Mahavavy du Sud drainage, NW Madagascar
notes: Re-confirmed at Lake Kinkony by Ramanantsalama et al. 2018 after concern about extirpation.

distribution_narrative: Nosy Be, Sambirano River, Sahinana/Sampiana Rivers (W Montagne d Ambre); range narrowed after P. loisellei and P. risengi split
notes: Type species of genus. IUCN status may need revision after P. loisellei and P. risengi splits.

distribution_narrative: Amboaboa River / Mangarahara confluence, Sofia drainage, NW Madagascar
notes: 1 individual captured in 2024 Fusari survey — confirms extant. Amboaboa is KBA for 3 CR species. Listed under cf. kieneri on some CARES lists.

distribution_narrative: Formerly Lake Kinkony (extirpated); Lake Ravelobe, Lake Tseny, Kalamilotra River, Lake Amparinandrina, Betsiboka/Mahajamba basins
captive_institutions: Leipzig Zoo; Bronx Zoo; private keepers in 6 countries
notes: Extirpated from type locality (Lake Kinkony).

distribution_narrative: Lower Mahajamba River, Lake Tseny, Anjingo River near Antsohihy
notes: = CARES sp. giant lamena (CARES list needs updating). Anjingo R. is new locality from Simkova et al. 2019 barcoding.

distribution_narrative: Mahanara River, ~4km NW Antsirabe-Nord, NE Madagascar; three precise paratype localities
captive_institutions: Cologne Zoo (50 offspring); Toronto Zoo (85 offspring)
notes: = CARES sp. ventitry (CARES list needs updating). Named for Paul V. Loiselle (1947-2025). Only eastern Paretroplus apart from P. polyactis.

distribution_narrative: Betsiboka River drainage; Ikopa River basin; Lake Ravelobe (apparently extirpated Oct 2024)
captive_institutions: ZIMS + private keepers; Aspinall Foundation active
notes: Possibly extinct in wild. Aspinall Foundation Oct 2024: extirpated from Lake Ravelobe. 2011 Ikopa River barcoding record (RDR0251 at -17.0241, 48.7599) may be most recent wild locality.

distribution_narrative: Andranomalaza drainage near Maromandia; Maevarano River; Maintsomalaza River; formerly Lake Andrapongy (extirpated)
notes: Extirpated from Lake Andrapongy.

distribution_narrative: Lake Tseny (only surviving wild population), Sofia River basin, NW Madagascar; formerly Lake Sarodrano and Bemarivo floodplain lakes
captive_institutions: ZSL London Zoo; Bolton Museum; hobbyists; 7 European countries + North America
notes: Rediscovered Oct 2010 at Lake Tseny (Denver Zoo/Madagasikara Voakajy). Extinct at type locality (Lake Sarodrano).

distribution_narrative: Mangarahara River and Amboaboa River, Sofia drainage, NW Madagascar
notes: 37 individuals captured in 2024 Fusari survey at Amboaboa River (-15.9517, 48.7233).

distribution_narrative: Betsiboka River drainage; Maintimaso River and Lake Ambanja (inferred by Sparks 2008)
notes: 22 barcoded specimens in Vences et al. 2022 labeled P. petiti from hobbyist stock — taxonomic identity needs verification.

distribution_narrative: Eastern coast of Madagascar, ~14S-25S; Maroantsetra, Ambila, Manombo, Canal des Pangalanes, Farafangana
notes: Only eastern-coast Paretroplus (plus P. loisellei in NE). Pangalanes at Andevoranto: -18.955, 49.108.

distribution_narrative: Anjingo-Ankofia drainage including Lake Andrapongy, NW Madagascar
notes: NEW SPECIES 2025. Named for Karen J. Riseng. Sister to P. damii + P. loisellei. Healthy populations reported.

distribution_narrative: Kalamilotra River, Betsiboka River basin, NW Madagascar
notes: Biparental substrate spawner. ~16S, 47E approximate.

distribution_narrative: Sofia River basin near Mandritsara; Lake Tseny population may be distinct
notes: Distinguished by small body size and unusual rostral profile. ~15.83S, 48.83E approximate.

distribution_narrative: Western slope of Montagne d Ambre; Mananjeba River (Lake Babano), Mahavavy du Nord, Ifasy River
notes: Part of P. damii complex. Captive stock from Lake Babano collected 1999 by Nourissat. ~13.20S, 49.03E approximate.

distribution_narrative: Lake Amparihinandrina, likely lower Betsiboka drainage, NW Madagascar
notes: Biggest Paretroplus data gap. No published literature, no GPS. Name similarity to P. kieneri Lake Amparinandrina record — may be same waterbody.

distribution_narrative: Riviere Andranofanjava west of Amboitra; also Diana Region (Sapiana River)
notes: All GBIF records are museum specimens without coordinates (MHNG). Diana Region record may indicate wider distribution.

distribution_narrative: Mananara du Nord River at Antanibaolina village, 108m ASL, NE Madagascar
notes: 2 GBIF records at -16.25, 49.678 (2006-10-04, P.V. Loiselle). May correspond to CARES sp. mananara.

distribution_narrative: Eastern and southeastern coast from Mananjary to Anosy Region (Fort Dauphin); Manombo Reserve
notes: 8 GBIF locality records + DNA barcoding. CARES=CVU vs IUCN=LC discrepancy.

distribution_narrative: Anjingo-Ankofia drainage, Antsohihy region, NW Madagascar; Lake Andrapongy North Basin
notes: 6 GBIF records. Type at -14.8447, 48.2439. Micro-endemic.

distribution_narrative: Amboaboa River, Mangarahara tributary, Sofia drainage, NW Madagascar
captive_institutions: Cologne Zoo; Toronto Zoo; ZSL London
notes: Mangarahara Cichlid. Confirmed extant 2024. 215+ fry distributed by Cologne Zoo. Probable GBIF record at -15.8336, 48.7142.

distribution_narrative: Mahanara River, ~4km NW Antsirabe-Nord, SAVA Region, NE Madagascar
notes: Citizen Conservation species. Named for Paul Loiselle. GBIF at -13.95, 49.933 and -13.98, 49.952.

distribution_narrative: Fort Dauphin (Tolanaro) region, SE Madagascar
notes: No georeferenced GBIF records. Known only from type series.

distribution_narrative: Antainambalana River near Maroantsetra, NE Madagascar
notes: Rediscovered Sept 2023 in Makira Natural Park; lost since 2003.

distribution_narrative: Widespread: Nosy Be lakes, east coast (Pangalanes to Manombo), NW (Mananjeba, Sambirano), central highlands; 361 GBIF records
notes: Most widespread and best-documented Ptychochromis. Breeding guidelines published Dec 2022. 205 georeferenced GBIF records.

distribution_narrative: Formerly Onilahy River system, SW Madagascar
notes: EXTINCT. Not seen since 1962. Described posthumously from Kiener specimens.

distribution_narrative: Ilanana River near Ranohira south of Isalo NP; historically Ankafaua (Betsileo), Maroamalona
notes: Only 1 GBIF record with coords: -22.6833, 45.35. Northern populations almost certainly extinct.

distribution_narrative: Lake Itasy and surrounding waters, central Madagascar highlands
notes: Presumed extinct from type locality. Lake severely degraded by invasives.

distribution_narrative: Mananara du Sud River basin near Vondrozo/Vevembe, SE Madagascar
notes: 5 GBIF records with coords, all within ~3km radius. Type at -22.8143, 47.1413.

distribution_narrative: Mananara du Nord region, NE Madagascar
notes: May correspond to or overlap with P. ernestmagnusi (Allgayer 2019).

distribution_narrative: Multiple eastern drainages from Mananara du Nord (NE) to Mahampanihm River near Fort Dauphin (SE)
notes: Most widespread anchariid. 10+ localities. Southernmost: -24.658, 46.870.

distribution_narrative: Onilahy River drainage (Ihazofotsy and Manana Rivers), near Isalo NP, SW Madagascar
notes: Only western-draining anchariid. Type at -22.982, 47.369. Only 3 known specimens.

distribution_narrative: Andriambondro River / Sandrananta drainage, SE highlands
notes: Type at -22.363, 47.368. Only 2 known specimens. Micro-endemic. Unique concave dorsal fin.

distribution_narrative: Mananara du Nord River at Antonibaolina village, 108m ASL, NE Madagascar
notes: Most precisely documented anchariid type locality: -16.3132, 49.6645, 108m. Only 4 preserved specimens. Leiss et al. recommended upgrade to VU.

distribution_narrative: Mananjary River basin headwaters near Ambohimango, E Madagascar
notes: Possibly least-collected freshwater fish in Madagascar. Only holotype (1896). Type at -20.867, 47.600. Unique bicuspid teeth.

distribution_narrative: Mangoro River and Nosivolo River drainages, E Madagascar
notes: Type species of genus Gogo. Type at -20.006, 48.770. Highly mottled pigmentation. Nosivolo = first Ramsar river site in Madagascar (2010).

distribution_narrative: Basins of Mahavavy du Sud, Betsiboka, Lake Kinkony basin, Tsingy de Bemaraha; NW Madagascar
notes: Most literature since 1950 referring to P. omalonotus in western Madagascar actually refers to P. arnoulti. Extirpated from Maevatanana by Xiphophorus hellerii.

distribution_narrative: Nosy Be island and Sambirano River basin on adjacent mainland; Ankify peninsula
captive_institutions: 6 European countries per Leiss et al. 2022
notes: NOT broadly distributed across western Madagascar as previously believed — those populations are P. arnoulti. Color polymorphism (yellow/red/blue morphs).

distribution_narrative: Basins of Mananjeba, Mahavavy du Nord, Ifasy, Manehoko, Ampandra rivers, NW Madagascar
notes: Type at -13.4150, 48.8389 (Ampandra River). Named for Patricia Griffith Yazgi.

distribution_narrative: Sakaramy River and upper Antongombato River, extreme N Madagascar; 500m asl
captive_institutions: Cologne Zoo; Zoo Zurich; 510 individuals in European zoos
notes: One of rarest freshwater fish in world. Ziegler et al. 2020 recommended upgrade to CR. Extirpated from most aboriginal range since 1928.

distribution_narrative: Ankofia River and Anjingo tributary streams, NW Madagascar
notes: Type at -14.8722, 48.2478 (Bentainkilotra Creek). Part of Bora Special Nature Reserve.

distribution_narrative: Basins of Menambery, Fanambana, and Ampanobe rivers, NE Madagascar (eastern slope of Tsaratanana Massif)
notes: First Pachypanchax on eastern slope of Madagascar. Varatraza = east wind. Channa maculata wiped out lower-reach populations.

distribution_narrative: Sofia River basin headwaters, Amboaboa area, NW Madagascar
notes: 44 specimens captured in Carvalho et al. 2024 survey. Conservation breeding ponds managed by APPA. Replaces P. arnoulti in Sofia basin.

distribution_narrative: Widespread in Madagascar freshwaters
Forested region of Mahambo, NE Madagascar
notes: Possibly extinct.

distribution_narrative: Cave systems, Mahafaly Plateau, SW Madagascar: Mitoho sinkhole (type), Andranomalaza, Andranoilovy, Andriamaniloke, Ambilahilalika wells, Nikotsy sinkhole
notes: Most widely distributed Typhleotris. Cryptic species suspected (Vences et al. 2018). Hybridization with T. mararybe at Lalia sinkhole.

distribution_narrative: Grotte de Vitane near Itampolo, plus 4 additional sinkholes, Mahafaly Plateau, SW Madagascar
notes: Only darkly pigmented obligate cavefish. Type at -24.7019, 43.9642. Hybridization with T. pauliani at Tehafe sinkhole. SHOAL 1000 Priority Fish.

distribution_narrative: Morombe coastal karst, SW Madagascar; Grotte d Andranomaly (type), sinkholes from ~22.07S to ~22.32S
notes: Range entirely north of Onilahy River; separate karst system from T. madagascariensis. Most range outside protected areas.

distribution_narrative: Formerly from forested swamps near Mahambo, NE coastal Madagascar (~17.49S, 49.47E)
notes: EXTINCT. Reclassified Meinema & Huber 2023 / Carr, Martin & Sparks 2024.

distribution_narrative: Pandanus swamp in Reserve Speciale de Manombo, SE coastal Madagascar (~23.02S, 47.73E)
notes: NEW SPECIES 2024. Presumed extinct. Am. Mus. Novitates 4012. Honahona = swampy in Malagasy.

distribution_narrative: Amboaboa River near Mangarahara confluence, Sofia drainage, NW Madagascar
notes: IUCN still uses Arius festinus. Type at ~-15.8336, 48.7142. Freshwater rapids specialist. Marceniuk et al. 2023 genus transfer.

distribution_narrative: Lake Andrapongy, Ankofia River drainage, NW Madagascar (~-14.6969, 48.1317)
notes: IUCN still uses Arius uncinatus. Lacustrine micro-endemic. Marceniuk et al. 2023 genus transfer.

distribution_narrative: Widespread in Madagascar freshwaters
Amboaboa River area, NW Madagascar

Highland and eastern slope freshwaters; Mantadia National Park, E Madagascar
notes: Monotypic genus. IUCN status disputed: prompt says DD, some sources say EN — verify directly.
